symptoms.symptom_name: Transient, migratory, intensely pruritic raised weals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weals that migrate and each resolve within 24 hours are the defining feature of urticaria.
Central dermal oedema with surrounding vasodilation produces the classic pale-centred weal; patient/video-describable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermatographism on firm stroking of unaffected skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inducible weal-and-flare on stroking supports mast-cell hyper-releasability; a clinician manoeuvre, sealed as answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent mild viral upper-respiratory illness 4 days prior

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Viral infection is a common non-allergenic trigger of acute spontaneous urticaria, especially when no food/drug trigger is found.

symptoms.symptom_name: No angioedema, airway, respiratory, circulatory or GI involvement (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative multi-system screen is what excludes anaphylaxis and confirms skin-limited urticaria — the single most important step in this case.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (BP 118/76, HR 88, RR 14, Temp 36.9, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal perfusion and oxygenation argue against distributive shock; clinician-measured vitals, sealed. The AI infers stability from the absence of reported faintness/breathlessness, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest clear to auscultation bilaterally with equal air entry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No bronchospasm on auscultation; clinician examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No relief from standard-dose loratadine 10 mg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of a single standard antihistamine dose does not change the diagnosis but drives the management step of up-dosing rather than repeating the same dose.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Migratory pale-centred pruritic weals together with a negative multi-system red-flag screen confirm benign, skin-limited acute urticaria and exclude anaphylaxis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in a first acute episode with no identifiable allergen trigger; reserved for chronic or recurrent urticaria under specialist guidance. Ordering it acutely is an error of commission.
Tryptase supports anaphylaxis/mast-cell activation; there is no anaphylaxis here, so it is not indicated.
No systemic features; routine bloods are not indicated in straightforward acute urticaria and would be over-investigation.
Only warranted if urticarial vasculitis is suspected (weals lasting >24h, painful, bruising) — those features are absent here.
